name: Bechamel Sauce
recipeIngredient: salt and white pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a cut into the onion about 1 inch deep and slide the bay leaf into this slit.
2. Stick the cloves into the onion.
3. Place the onion, along with the milk, nutmeg, and bay leaf, into a heavy-gauge, non-corrosive saucepan.
4. Heat over medium heat.
5. In a separate pan, cook the butter and flour for about 5 minutes, stirring continuously, without browning, until it emits a nutty aroma. Remove from the heat.
6. When the milk is fairly hot, slowly pour some into the cooled-down roux while stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
7. Return the remaining milk to the pan and simmer gently for 15 minutes, stirring frequently.
8. Season with salt and white pepper to taste. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve before serving.
Warning: Bechamel sauce tends to form a skin on top due to air exposure. To prevent this, dab a piece of butter onto the surface using a fork after cooking.
